channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers no additional benefits in muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity compared to conventional low-load exercise (LLE) or high-load exercise (HLE) for individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A prospective, randomized controlled study in a Swiss ED found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between advanced physiotherapists and resident physicians compared to senior physicians for musculoskeletal dysfunctions, supporting APP roles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study involving 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults compared six ActiGraph accelerometers and pressure insoles for step count accuracy against an instrumented treadmill. It found that accuracy varied across walking speeds and sensor locations.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) grouped into three domains (functioning, general well-being, and social/environmental factors) and corresponding measures (PROMs) was developed for pediatric physical therapy through focus groups, interviews, a scoping review, and expert panel assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis of 62,217 Medicare beneficiaries found that mandatory bundled payment for lower extremity joint replacement significantly affected the delivery of preoperative rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: An editorial provides an update to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, emphasizing recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis identified the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across various training levels and assessment methods, also identifying moderators associated with these prevalence estimates.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: This systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis (EDSS ≤6.0), confirming its positive influence.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: This RCT (n=140) found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program improved 6-minute walk test distance and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity at 6 months post-TIA or mild stroke, compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cross-Education Effect on Force Production Capacity After Unilateral Resistance Training in Clinical Populations: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review with meta-analysis established the cross-education effect after unilateral resistance training (CE-URT) in clinical populations, including those with musculoskeletal conditions and hemiparesis after stroke, demonstrating force production gains in contralateral non-trained muscles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-Effectiveness of Post-Acute Stroke Rehabilitation From a U.S. Healthcare Payer Perspective: Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities Versus Skilled Nursing Facilities.
item.description: This decision analytic model evaluated the cost-effectiveness of inpatient rehabilitation facilities (IRFs) versus skilled nursing facilities (SNFs) for stroke survivors in the US, showing IRFs to be more cost-effective from a payer perspective by simulating 90-day outcomes, including mortality and community discharge.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved 70.9% completion rates—exceeding national benchmarks (43%)—and 96% of completers achieved Minimal Clinically Important Differences in outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cognitive Rehabilitation Improves Functional Outcomes in Adults With Post-COVID Cognitive Impairment: Study
item.description: A randomized clinical trial found that cognitive rehabilitation led to significantly greater and sustained improvements in functional goal attainment compared to usual care in adults with post-COVID cognitive impairment.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (1,372 participants) found that shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.02, -0.42]; p < .01) in knee osteoarthritis patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review comparing upper limb rehabilitation strategies in chronic stroke patients with and without contralateral C7 nerve transfer identified significant evidence gaps regarding rehabilitation dose, content, and delivery models between technology-assisted rehabilitation and CC7 studies.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A multi-method summit-Delphi consensus process involving 83 participants from seven governing bodies of tennis and the broader ecosystem generated recommendations for mental healthcare provision and wellness promotion among elite tennis athletes, including juniors.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients determined subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking at discharge after inpatient stroke rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective surveillance study monitored 4,450 athletes during the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games, reporting 535 injuries (incidence of 8.6 per 1000 athlete days) and highlighting Taekwondo (27.3) and football 5-a-side (22.3) as high-risk sports.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder is a generative foundation model of walking kinematics for diverse clinical applications and pathologies, enabling actionable insights from smartphone video-based gait analysis using eight aggregated gait datasets.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A systematic review of 22 studies identified psychological and social impacts as key psychosocial effects of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, affecting 645 informants.
item.author: PubMed
